title: Claude Dispatch Just Dropped, And It Kills OpenClaw
summary: Claude Dispatch is Anthropic's new feature for agentic workflows through the Claude app. It provides a more secure alternative to OpenClaw by running autonomous tasks directly within Claude rather than through third-party platforms like Telegram.
writeup: Claude Dispatch is Anthropic's native feature that connects a phone directly to a local Claude Code instance, letting users invoke pre-built agent skills remotely without leaving their subscription. The video walks through three live demonstrations on a phone: scraping 200 business leads, running an inbox cleaner that pre-drafts replies, and generating a thumbnail, all in parallel. The through-line is that DIY Telegram-bot agent stacks expose API keys and billing users at raw API rates (roughly 4 to 8x more expensive than a subscription), whereas Dispatch runs locally behind a per-request permission allowlist and counts against a flat monthly charge.
key_points: Claude Dispatch routes phone commands to a locally running Claude Code process, so the agent runs on the user's own machine rather than a third-party server, preserving file and MCP connector access without exposing credentials to external services.

Third-party Telegram-style agent bridges such as Open Claw have leaked tens of thousands of API keys and bill at direct API pricing, which the presenter estimates ran to roughly $100 in a few days of casual use versus a subsidized subscription that is 4 to 8x cheaper.

The security model is an explicit per-request permission allowlist: when a running skill needs access to a resource it was not pre-authorized for, a prompt surfaces on the phone and the user approves or denies before execution continues, replacing implicit ambient credential exposure.

Three skills ran in parallel during the demo: a lead scraper returning 200 California agency owners with phone numbers, an inbox cleaner that marked solicitation email as read and pre-drafted replies to important messages, and a thumbnail generator with face-swap functionality, all initiated from a phone and reported back to it.

At recording time Dispatch was live on Max plans with Anthropic stating intent to extend it to Pro plans, positioning it as the sanctioned, subscription-bundled path for running agentic workflows from mobile rather than a separate product or API tier.
